assertions.title: User Registration - Register a new user, verify user is created in database with hashed password

steps.action: Query database: SELECT id, username, password_hash FROM users WHERE username='testuser_001'
steps.expected: password_hash field contains argon2 hash (starts with $argon2id), not plain text

assertions.title: User Login - Login with valid credentials, verify JWT token is returned

steps.observed: access_token returned (64-char bearer token), token_type=bearer, 200 status

assertions.title: Invalid Login Rejection - Login with wrong password, verify error is returned (401) and no token is issued

steps.observed: {"detail":"Invalid username or password"}, 401 status, no access_token

assertions.title: Protected Routes - Call GET /api/auth/me without token (expect 401), then with valid token (expect 200 + user info)

steps.action: Login to get token, then GET /api/auth/me with Bearer token

assertions.title: Password Encryption at Rest - Query database directly and verify password_hash contains argon2 hash, not plain text

steps.action: sqlite3 query: SELECT password_hash FROM users WHERE username='testuser_001'
steps.expected: password_hash starts with $argon2id (argon2), not $2a$ (bcrypt), and is not plain text

evidence.dbQuery: SELECT password_hash FROM users WHERE username='testuser_001'

summary: Tested 5 assertions (VAL-AUTH-001 through VAL-AUTH-005): all passed. User registration creates users with argon2-hashed passwords in the database. Login returns JWT tokens. Invalid credentials return 401. Protected routes require authentication. Password hashes in DB are argon2id format, not plain text.
